title: Slope
notes: Slope represents the rate of change between two points.
You may use any two points on the line.
Be consistent: if you do y2 − y1 on top, you must do x2 − x1 on the bottom.
Positive slopes rise as you move to the right; negative slopes fall.
Undefined slope occurs when the line is vertical (x-values are equal).
applications: Interpreting the steepness of a line.
Understanding rate of change in real-world contexts like speed, cost, temperature, and population.
Determining whether relationships are increasing or decreasing.
